qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authentication on WebRPC administrative API allows unauthorized sector operations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated WebRPC administrative JSON-RPC operations, while the advisory targets raw pgx/database error propagation and unmasked connection strings in PDP, mk12, and mk20 HTTP error paths.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: NullAuth default configuration allows unauthenticated access to all PDP services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets PDP NullAuth accepting unauthenticated callers as public, while the advisory targets authenticated HTTP handlers exposing raw database errors and connection credentials.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: database credentials exposed through raw pgx error messages, http.Error(err.Error()) paths in PDP/mk12/mk20 handlers, or plaintext passwords embedded in database connection strings.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gj6x-q8rh-wj6x describes Curio credential disclosure through verbose HTTP error responses and unmasked database connection strings.
vuln_webrpc_missing_auth reports missing authentication on /api/webrpc/v0 administrative JSON-RPC routes, not database error disclosure.
vuln_pdp_nullauth_bypass reports NullAuth default authentication bypass for PDP endpoints, not raw pgx error propagation or connection-string leakage.
